fact: November 10 is exactly 314 days into the year - that's the same as the first three digits of pi!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels go absolutely bonkers burying nuts, sometimes hiding up to 10,000 acorns in a single season!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most of the northern United States around this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework.
Scorpio season is in full swing on November 10, and people born under this sign are supposed to be the most mysterious of all the zodiac signs.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 10 marks the start of serious spring heat - perfect beach weather while the north gets chilly!
Many deciduous trees are completely bare by this date in cold regions, looking like giant skeletons reaching toward the sky.
The first frost of winter typically hits many northern areas right around this time, turning morning grass into millions of tiny ice crystals.
November 10 falls almost exactly halfway between the autumn equinox and winter solstice in the Northern Hemisphere.
Deer are usually in their mating season around this date in North America, which makes them act super weird and sometimes run into roads without looking.
In many northern places, November 10 is often when you can first see your breath in the morning air, creating little puff clouds like a tiny dragon.
Migrating geese are typically flying overhead in huge V-formations around this time in North America, honking so loudly they can wake you up at dawn.
